rankings.writeup: Team Nathan is the clear frontrunner through two weeks, sitting as the only undefeated squad in the league. Nathan Paik is putting up monster double-double numbers (26.3 PPG, 11.7 RPG) and Austin Im has been a lethal perimeter threat, erupting for 30 points on 6-for-6 from three against Sean. Add in Ryan Choi's sharpshooting and Andrew Paik's rebounding, and this team has the deepest rotation in the OBA. Their 81-60 dismantling of Sean in Week 2 was the most dominant performance of the season so far.

Team Sean showed two very different faces in the first two weeks. The Week 1 win over Matt was gritty — Sean Lee poured in 25 points and Christian Kim grabbed 15 rebounds to grind out a 55-50 victory. But the 21-point blowout loss to Nathan exposed some depth concerns. Christian Kim's 29-point, 10-rebound double-double in the loss was a silver lining, proving he can be a legitimate star. If Josh Paik can find his shooting stroke, this team has the pieces to compete with anyone.

Team Aron bounced back from a tough Week 1 loss to Nathan with a convincing 57-43 win over PJo. The key? Aron Kim stepping up with a 13-point, 13-rebound double-double, while Jonathan Chung led the scoring with 16 points. Josh Sun remains the most versatile player on the roster — he does a little bit of everything with 4 steals and 2 blocks against PJo. The defense looked elite in Week 2, holding PJo to just 43 points. If they can sustain that intensity, they'll be dangerous come playoff time.

It's hard to draw too many conclusions from one game, but Team Matt's 50-55 loss to Sean raised some questions about shot selection. Matt Paik went 5-for-21 from the field and the team shot poorly from three overall. The bright spot was David Kim, who was an efficient 5-for-7 with 10 points and 7 rebounds off the bench. Kanghee Lee showed flashes with 13 points and 7 boards. This team has talent — they just need to find their rhythm and get cleaner looks.

Team PJo has been competitive but can't seem to close games. The 67-68 heartbreaker against Nathan in Week 1 could have gone either way — Joseph Yoo dropped 22 points and PJo had a 21-point, 14-rebound double-double in a losing effort. But the Week 2 loss to Aron (43-57) was more concerning, as the offense sputtered despite Wonjin Jang's 17-point, 10-rebound outing. The talent is there with guys like Justin Ho and Joseph Yoo, but they need to find consistency and a way to finish close games.
